title: centaurea × baumgarteniana
intro: Centaurea × baumgarteniana, also known as Baumgarten's knapweed, is a hybrid plant species within the Asteraceae family, derived from the cross between Centaurea montana and Centaurea cyanus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Centaurea × baumgarteniana is often prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to take advantage of its potential medicinal properties.
